subject: Alaska.Ai — LinkedIn Carousel No. 5 — 2026-07-12 — First Machine to the Fire
html_body:
Alaska.Ai · LinkedIn Carousel No. 5

2026-07-12 · 9 slides · branch tsturg/ecstatic-cerf-c5zog5

1 · Upload the document

Download carousel.pdf (2.3 MB, vector text) → LinkedIn → start a post → add a document → upload the PDF → title it: First Machine to the Fire

2 · Paste the post copy

Five teams are racing across 1,000 square kilometers of Alaska to prove a machine can fight a wildfire with no human in the loop.

This is the XPRIZE Wildfire autonomous track, an 11 million dollar contest field-tested through the University of Alaska Fairbanks. The winner is expected in September 2026.

The deck covers the Alaska test range, the five finalists cut from nearly 300 teams, the loop each machine has to close from spark to suppression, the prize money, and the caveat that keeps getting skipped: a contest winner is not the same as proof.

Would you trust an autonomous machine to reach an Interior fire before a crew could, or does that claim need a full Alaska summer first?

#AlaskaWildfire #AutonomousDrones #XPRIZEWildfire

3 · Post, then paste this as the FIRST comment (within a minute)

Sources:
XPRIZE Wildfire (finalists, tracks, prize structure, ACUASI, $11M purse): https://www.xprize.org/news/meet-the-future-of-autonomous-wildfire-response-xprize-wildfire-announces-finalist-teams-advancing-in-11m-competition
Dryad Networks, self-reported (Alaska demo, up to 100 L, sensor count, nearly 300 teams, September 2026): https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260707802818/en/Dryad-Networks-Reaches-XPRIZE-Wildfire-Finals-Milestone-Demonstrating-Autonomous-Detection-and-Suppression-of-Wildfires-in-Alaska
FAA UAS Test Sites (ACUASI designation): https://www.faa.gov/uas/programs_partnerships/test_sites
ASU News (Alaska fire-season background): https://news.asu.edu/20251028-science-and-technology-using-ai-help-predict-wildfires-alaska
Every Alaska AI decision we track, with live deadlines, lives at the Alaska AI Docket: https://alaskaaihq.com

Editor's note

No hard fails; ships at 8.54 vs 8.3. Honesty posture is the deck's spine: the Dryad detect-and-suppress demo (S6) carries 'Dryad says' plus a COMPANY SELF REPORT / NOT INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIED caveat; the fire-season stats (S3) are labeled BACKGROUND with the 'nearly half' figure tied to 2022; all five finalists are shown equal with no 'final three'. Weakest area is the rendered S6 hero reading flatter than the GPU-PBR ambition; parked for Phase 12.

Automation changes this run (2)

The routine modified its own machinery this run. If a later week looks worse, this dated section is the rollback trail (each set reverts as one commit).
KindChangeRollback
fix
assets		New akthree helper AKT.objectHero(R, group, {toward, keyColor, intensity, height}) (plus standalone AKT.fitHeight): scales a foreground hero to a target world height (the framing bump) and adds a separation rim light on the FAR side of the subject from the camera, leaned toward the key direction, aimed at the subject center, so a dark object reads as a carved SILHOUETTE against a dark background instead of a flat blob. Documented in SKILL.md under the akthree bench. Opt-in; existing scenes unaffected.
why: Run 2026-07-12 weakest scorer criterion (Artwork craft & genuine detail = 7): the S6 akthree object hero (backlit quadcopter) rendered as a blob under key+fill+ambient and only read after a warm r		revert the upgrade(2026-07-12) commit
upgrade(2026-07-12) commit 01a43fb (revert this single prefixed commit to roll back)
fix
prompts		Phase 3 and Phase 10 of the master routine now state explicitly that the fact-checker and scorer subagents have NO Write tool by design and return their JSON in the final message, which the showrunner persists to claims.json / score_report.json (replacing the '-> out/<date>/claims.json' and 'Save score_report.json' phrasing that implied the agent writes the file).
why: Run 2026-07-12 contract mismatch: the fact-checker (tools: WebFetch, Read) and scorer (tools: Read) cannot write files, so the showrunner had to persist their returned JSON by hand; the Phase 3/10 wor		revert the upgrade(2026-07-12) commit
upgrade(2026-07-12) commit 01a43fb (revert this single prefixed commit to roll back)

Aftercare (this is where reach is won)

  • Post Tuesday to Thursday, 8 to 11am Alaska time (the day signal is robust; the hour matters less).
  • Within about 60 seconds of posting, paste the sources block above as the FIRST comment.
  • Reply substantively to every comment in the first 60 to 90 minutes; threaded author replies drive the most reach.
  • Do not edit the post in the first hour, since edits can suppress reach.
  • Judge this deck on saves first, then comments, then shares, then likes, against our trailing median.
